name: Identity Fusion NG
description: Identity Fusion NG is a connector that allows you to manage Identity Fusion accounts by generating unique identifiers, preventing duplicate identities through similarity matching, and merging account attributes from multiple authoritative sources.

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure how this connector connects to Identity Security Cloud.

Use Identity Security Cloud API URL to specify the base URL of your ISC tenant (for example, https://tenant.api.identitynow.com).

Provide the Personal Access Token ID and Personal Access Token Secret for an account that has the required API permissions (Sources, Identities, Accounts, Workflows/Forms). These values are used to authenticate all connector operations.

items.label: Identity Security Cloud API URL
items.helpKey: Base URL of your Identity Security Cloud tenant (format: https://<tenant>.api.identitynow.com)

items.label: Personal Access Token ID
items.helpKey: Enter the Client ID from your Personal Access Token. The token must have permissions to manage sources, identities, accounts, and forms

items.label: Personal Access Token secret
items.helpKey: Enter the Client Secret from your Personal Access Token. This value is stored securely and should be rotated periodically

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Define which identities are in scope for processing.

Use Include identities in the scope? to include identities in addition to managed accounts from configured sources. This is useful for identity-only attribute generation or to define the baseline for deduplication.

Use Identity Scope Query to limit which identities are evaluated. This uses ISC search syntax (e.g. attributes.cloudLifecycleState:active, source.name:"Workday"). Only identities matching this query are considered.

items.label: Include identities in the scope?
items.helpKey: When enabled, existing identities are included alongside accounts from your configured sources. Turn this on if you want to generate attributes from identity data or use identities as a baseline for detecting duplicates

items.label: Identity Scope Query
items.helpKey: Filter which identities are included using a search query. Use * for all identities, or narrow results with filters like attributes.cloudLifecycleState:active or source.name:"Workday"

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Select the account sources that feed Identity Fusion.

Use Authoritative Account Sources to configure sources whose accounts will be merged. For each source:
  • Source name: Must match the ISC source name exactly.
  • Source type: Choose Authoritative, Record, or Orphan behavior for unmatched accounts.
  • Account aggregation: Choose to aggregate before processing, after a delay, or not at all. Optimized aggregation can be disabled for reverse correlation. Delimited text sources cannot be aggregated.
  • Correlation mode: Choose whether to correlate during processing, set up reverse correlation, or leave accounts uncorrelated.
  • Account filter: Limit which accounts are processed (uses ISC search syntax).
  • Aggregation batch size: Limit accounts per run (useful for initial loading).

Global settings control retries and wait times for aggregation task polling.

items.label: Source name
items.helpKey: Enter the exact name of the source as it appears in Identity Security Cloud (case-sensitive)

items.label: Enabled
items.helpKey: Turn off to temporarily exclude this source from processing without removing it

items.label: Source type
items.helpKey: Choose how accounts from this source are handled. 'Authoritative accounts' creates new Fusion accounts for unmatched entries (default). 'Records' processes accounts for attribute generation but does not create Fusion accounts for unmatched ones. 'Orphan accounts' skips unmatched accounts entirely, with an option to disable them

items.label: Disable non-matching accounts
items.helpKey: Automatically disable orphan accounts that no longer match any identity after aggregation

items.label: Account filter
items.helpKey: Optionally limit which accounts are processed using a search query. Example: attributes.department:"Engineering"

items.label: Aggregation batch size
items.helpKey: Limit the number of accounts processed per run. Leave empty to process all accounts. Useful when loading large datasets for the first time

items.label: Account aggregation mode
items.helpKey: Choose when to pull the latest accounts from this source. 'Aggregate before processing' refreshes accounts first. 'Delayed aggregation' refreshes after processing completes. 'Do not aggregate' uses existing account data. Note: delimited file sources do not support aggregation

items.label: Aggregation delay (minutes)
items.helpKey: How many minutes to wait after processing completes before refreshing accounts from this source

items.label: Optimized aggregation
items.helpKey: Only process accounts that have changed since the last run, improving performance. Disable this if you use reverse correlation, which requires all accounts to be reprocessed

items.label: Optimized aggregation
items.helpKey: Only process accounts that have changed since the last run, improving performance. Disable this if you use reverse correlation, which requires all accounts to be reprocessed

items.label: Correlation mode
items.helpKey: Choose how uncorrelated source accounts are linked to identities. 'Correlate missing accounts on aggregation' links them automatically during processing. 'Reverse correlation from managed source' creates/uses a dedicated identity attribute and correlation rule so ISC can match them. 'Do not correlate' leaves them unlinked

items.label: Correlation attribute display name
items.helpKey: Friendly display label for the reverse-correlation attribute shown in Identity Security Cloud

items.label: Correlation attribute name
items.helpKey: Internal name for the attribute used to link accounts via reverse correlation. This must be unique and cannot overlap mapped/generated attributes or existing source schema attributes

items.label: Aggregation task result retries
items.helpKey: How many times to check whether an aggregation has finished. Increase if aggregations take a long time to complete. Default: 5

items.label: Aggregation task result wait time (seconds)
items.helpKey: Seconds to wait between each aggregation status check. Default: 1

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Control how Identity Fusion accounts are maintained.

  • Maximum history messages: Limit history entries per account (default: 10).
  • Delete accounts...: Remove Fusion account when all source accounts are gone.
  • Force attribute refresh...: Force Normal-type attributes to refresh every run (expensive).
  • Skip accounts...: Skip processing if native identity attribute is missing.

items.label: Maximum history messages
items.helpKey: Number of processing history entries to keep per account. Older entries are removed automatically. Default: 10

items.label: Delete accounts with no authoritative accounts left?
items.helpKey: Automatically remove a Fusion account when all of its linked source accounts have been deleted. Useful for cleaning up after users leave the organization

items.label: Force attribute refresh on each aggregation?
items.helpKey: Recalculate all computed attributes on every run, even if no changes were detected. This may increase processing time but ensures values stay up to date

items.label: Skip accounts with missing ID?
items.helpKey: Skip accounts that are missing a unique identity value. These accounts will be logged so you can review them later

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Map and merge source attributes into the Fusion account.

Default attribute merge: Global strategy for combining values (First found, Keep a list, Concatenate).

Attribute Mapping: Configure per-attribute rules.
  • New attribute: Name on Fusion account.
  • Existing attributes: Source attributes to map.
  • Merge override: Override global strategy for this attribute (includes "Source name" to prefer a specific source).

items.label: Default attribute merge from multiple sources
items.helpKey: When multiple sources provide the same attribute, choose how to combine them. 'First found' uses the value from the highest-priority source. 'Keep a list' stores all values. 'Concatenate' joins values into a single text

items.label: New attribute
items.helpKey: Name for this attribute on the Fusion account. This will appear in the account schema

items.label: Existing attributes
items.helpKey: Source attribute names to pull values from. Names must match the source account attributes exactly (case-sensitive)

items.label: Default attribute merge from multiple sources
items.helpKey: Override the default merge strategy for this specific attribute. Choose 'Source name' to always prefer the value from a specific source

items.label: Source name
items.helpKey: Name of the source whose value should be used for this attribute when available

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Generate computed attributes using Velocity templates.

Normal attributes are recalculated on each aggregation when Refresh is enabled.

Expression context: Mapped account attributes, $Math, $Datefns, $AddressParse, $Normalize.
Options: Case transform, normalization, space removal, max length.

items.label: Normal Attribute Definitions
items.helpKey: Define attributes whose values are generated from templates using mapped account data

items.label: Attribute Name
items.helpKey: Name for this generated attribute on the Fusion account

items.label: Apache Velocity expression
items.helpKey: Velocity template that generates the attribute value. You can reference any mapped attribute by name. Example: #set($i=$firstname.substring(0, 1))$i$lastname produces 'jsmith' from 'John Smith'

items.label: Refresh on each aggregation?
items.helpKey: When enabled, the value is recalculated on every run. When disabled, the value is only set once

items.label: Case selection
items.helpKey: Convert the generated value to a specific letter case

items.label: Normalize special characters?
items.helpKey: Replace accented and special characters with their plain equivalents (e.g. e instead of e with acute)

items.label: Remove spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove all spaces from the generated value

items.label: Trim leading and trailing spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove extra spaces at the beginning and end of the generated value

items.label: Maximum length
items.helpKey: Limit the generated value to this many characters. Leave empty for no limit

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Generate unique attributes using Velocity templates with uniqueness enforcement.

Unique attribute values are preserved once generated and only recalculated on account reset/enable.

Expression context: Mapped account attributes, $UUID (auto-generated v4 UUID), $counter (incremental or collision-based), $Math, $Datefns, $AddressParse, $Normalize.

Collision handling: On duplicate, the connector appends a disambiguation counter ($counter). Enable Use incremental counter for sequential numbering instead.
UUID: Use $UUID in the expression for an auto-generated immutable UUID.
Max attempts: Limits retries to prevent infinite loops.

items.label: Maximum attempts for unique attribute generation
items.helpKey: Maximum number of tries to find a value that is not already taken. Increase if you have a very large number of existing accounts. Default: 20

items.label: Unique Attribute Definitions
items.helpKey: Define attributes that must have a unique value across all accounts, such as usernames or employee IDs

items.label: Attribute Name
items.helpKey: Name for this unique attribute on the Fusion account

items.label: Apache Velocity expression
items.helpKey: Velocity template that generates the unique value. Use $counter for an automatic number suffix (e.g. jsmith1, jsmith2) or $UUID for a random unique ID. Example: #set($i=$firstname.substring(0, 1))$i$lastname$counter

items.label: Minimum counter digits ($counter)
items.helpKey: Minimum number of digits for the counter, padded with leading zeros. For example, 3 digits produces 001, 002, etc.

items.label: Use incremental counter?
items.helpKey: Always assign the next number in sequence instead of only adding a number when there is a conflict. Useful for generating sequential IDs like employee numbers

items.label: Counter start value
items.helpKey: The first number the counter will use. For example, set to 1000 to start numbering from 1000

items.label: Case selection
items.helpKey: Convert the generated value to a specific letter case

items.label: Normalize special characters?
items.helpKey: Replace accented and special characters with their plain equivalents (e.g. e instead of e with acute)

items.label: Remove spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove all spaces from the generated value

items.label: Trim leading and trailing spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove extra spaces at the beginning and end of the generated value

items.label: Maximum length
items.helpKey: Limit the generated value to this many characters. Leave empty for no limit

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure duplicate identity detection.

Fusion attribute matches: Define attributes to compare.
  • Algorithm: Enhanced Name Matcher, Jaro-Winkler, LIG3, Dice, Double Metaphone.
  • Score: Similarity threshold (0-100).
  • Mandatory: Require this match?

Scoring mode: Use per-attribute thresholds (default) or single overall average score.
Auto-correlate: Automatically merge precise matches without manual review.

items.label: Attribute
items.helpKey: The identity attribute to compare when looking for duplicates (e.g. firstname, lastname, email)

items.label: Matching algorithm
items.helpKey: Algorithm used to compare values. 'Enhanced Name Matcher' is best for names. 'Jaro-Winkler' works well for short strings with typos. 'LIG3' handles international characters. 'Dice' suits longer text. 'Double Metaphone' matches by pronunciation

options.label: Enhanced Name Matcher
options.docLinkLabel: Learn more about Enhanced Name Matcher

options.label: LIG3
options.docLinkLabel: Learn more about LIG3 (based on Levenshtein distance)

options.label: Double Metaphone
options.docLinkLabel: Learn more about Double Metaphone algorithm

items.label: Similarity score [0-100]
items.helpKey: How similar two values must be to count as a match, from 0 (no similarity) to 100 (exact match). Higher values reduce false positives

items.label: Mandatory match?
items.helpKey: When enabled, this attribute must meet its similarity threshold for a match to be considered. If no attributes are marked as mandatory, all are treated as required

items.label: Use overall fusion similarity score for all attributes?
items.helpKey: Instead of requiring each attribute to meet its own threshold, use a single combined score across all attributes to determine a match

items.label: Similarity score [0-100]
items.helpKey: The minimum combined similarity score (0-100) needed to consider two records a match

items.label: Automatically correlate if identical?
items.helpKey: Automatically merge matching records without requiring manual review. Only enable this when you are confident your matching rules produce accurate results

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure manual review for potential duplicates.

  • Form attributes: Identity attributes shown to reviewers (e.g. name, email, department).
  • Expiration: Days before review form expires (default: 7).
  • Global reviewer: Add source owner as reviewer to all forms (ensures assignment).
  • Report: Email report to owner after aggregation.

items.label: List of Fusion account attributes to include in form
items.helpKey: Account attributes displayed on the review form to help reviewers compare potential duplicates. For example: name, email, department

items.label: Manual review expiration days
items.helpKey: Number of days a reviewer has to respond before the review form expires. Default: 7

items.label: Owner is global reviewer?
items.helpKey: Always add the source owner as a reviewer on every review form, ensuring someone is always assigned to review potential duplicates

items.label: Send report to owner on aggregation?
items.helpKey: Send a summary report to the source owner after each run, including potential duplicates, processing outcomes, and source-type breakdown metrics

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Advanced options for troubleshooting and performance tuning.

  • Reset accounts: Force rebuild from scratch (caution in production).
  • Managed accounts batch size: Controls how many uncorrelated managed accounts are processed per duplicate-detection batch. Lower values reduce peak memory usage; higher values can improve throughput.
  • Concurrency check: Prevent concurrent aggregations (default: enabled).
  • External logging: Send logs to an external service (Splunk, etc.) for monitoring and incident analysis.

Start with the default batch size and tune only if needed: reduce it when runs are memory-constrained, increase it when memory headroom is available and you want faster processing.

items.label: Reset accounts?
items.helpKey: Rebuild all Fusion accounts from scratch on the next run. Use with caution in production and remember to turn this off after it completes

items.label: Managed accounts batch size
items.helpKey: Number of uncorrelated managed accounts processed in each duplicate-detection batch. Lower values reduce peak memory usage and may improve stability in constrained environments; higher values can improve throughput at the cost of more memory. Typical tuning range: 25-100. Default: 50

items.label: Enable concurrency check?
items.helpKey: Prevent multiple aggregations from running at the same time, which could cause conflicts. Recommended to keep enabled

items.label: Enable external logging?
items.helpKey: Send connector logs to an external service (e.g. Splunk) for centralized monitoring and troubleshooting

items.label: External logging URL
items.helpKey: The URL where logs should be sent (e.g. your Splunk or logging service endpoint)

items.label: External logging level
items.helpKey: Choose the detail level for external logs. 'Error' sends only critical issues. 'Debug' sends everything, which is useful for troubleshooting but produces more data

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Fine-tune API and queue behavior.

  • Provisioning timeout: Max wait for operations (default: 300s).
  • Queue: Enable rate limiting and concurrency control.
  • Retry: Auto-retry failed requests (recommended).
  • Processing wait time: Keep-alive interval for long operations.
  • Batching: Group requests for efficiency.

items.label: Provisioning timeout (seconds)
items.helpKey: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for a provisioning operation to complete. Increase this if you experience timeouts with large batches. Default: 300

items.label: Enable queue?
items.helpKey: Manage the flow of API requests to avoid overloading the server. Controls how many requests run at once and how fast they are sent

items.label: Maximum concurrent requests
items.helpKey: How many requests can run at the same time. Lower values are safer for rate-limited APIs. Default: 8

items.label: Requests per second
items.helpKey: Maximum number of requests sent per second. Reduce this if you are getting rate-limit errors. Default: 8

items.label: Enable retry?
items.helpKey: Automatically retry requests that fail due to temporary errors (e.g. timeouts or rate limits). Recommended for production use

items.label: API request retries
items.helpKey: How many times to retry a failed request before giving up. Default: 20

items.label: Processing wait time (seconds)
items.helpKey: How often (in seconds) to send a progress signal during long operations to prevent timeouts. Default: 60

items.label: Retry delay (milliseconds)
items.helpKey: Time to wait (in milliseconds) before retrying a failed request. The server may override this with its own recommended wait time. Default: 1000

items.label: Enable batching?
items.helpKey: Group multiple requests together to improve performance during large operations

items.label: Batch size
items.helpKey: Number of requests to group in each batch. Default: 250

items.label: Enable priority processing?
items.helpKey: Process critical operations (like saving data) before less urgent ones (like logging). Recommended for best performance

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Delegate processing to external server.

Run connector logic on your own infrastructure.
  • Proxy URL: External endpoint.
  • Proxy password: Secret for authentication (must match PROXY_PASSWORD env var on proxy).

items.label: Enable proxy mode?
items.helpKey: Route all processing through your own server instead of running it in the cloud. Use this if you need to run the connector on your own infrastructure

items.label: Proxy URL
items.helpKey: The URL of your proxy server that will handle connector operations

items.label: Proxy password
items.helpKey: A shared secret used to authenticate with your proxy server. This must match the password configured on the proxy

attributes.name: originSource
attributes.description: Origin source name (set on creation, immutable)
